proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.174 on pembrolizumab (n=25008) vs 0.164 off (n=24992); absolute difference +0.010 (chi-square p=0.003286); logistic regression OR=1.073, p=0.00329.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is larger in patients with PD-L1 TPS >= 0.50 (high) than in patients with PD-L1 TPS < 0.50 (low).

analyses.result_summary: In PD-L1 high (TPS>=0.50): pembro 0.209 vs no-pembro 0.156, diff +0.054, p=6.452e-13. In PD-L1 low: pembro 0.164 vs no-pembro 0.166, diff -0.002, p=0.5831. Interaction coefficient on log-odds = +0.376, p=5.037e-11.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is larger in tmb_high=1 patients than in tmb_high=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: In TMB-high: pembro 0.197 vs no-pembro 0.167, diff +0.030, p=4.863e-06. In TMB-low: pembro 0.165 vs no-pembro 0.163, diff +0.002, p=0.602. Interaction term log-odds = +0.184, p=0.0004146.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_sotorasib increases objective_response overall.
The benefit of treatment_sotorasib on objective_response is concentrated in kras_g12c=1 patients (interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Overall pembro? sotorasib: response 0.169 on vs 0.169 off, diff +0.001, p=0.8197.
In KRAS G12C+: sotorasib 0.175 vs no-sotorasib 0.166, diff +0.008, p=0.4097. In KRAS G12C-: sotorasib 0.169 vs no-sotorasib 0.169, diff -0.000, p=0.9429. Interaction log-odds = +0.059, p=0.4266.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_olaparib increases objective_response overall.
The benefit of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is concentrated in brca2_mutation=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Olaparib overall: response 0.170 on vs 0.169 off, diff +0.001, p=0.714.
In BRCA2+: olaparib 0.146 vs no-olaparib 0.168, diff -0.022, p=0.308. In BRCA2-: olaparib 0.171 vs no-olaparib 0.169, diff +0.002, p=0.5914. Interaction log-odds = -0.177, p=0.2747.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_osimertinib increases objective_response overall.
The benefit of treatment_osimertinib on objective_response is concentrated in egfr_mutation=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Osimertinib overall: response 0.165 on vs 0.170 off, diff -0.005, p=0.1751.
In EGFR+: osimertinib 0.164 vs no-osimertinib 0.166, diff -0.002, p=0.8414. In EGFR-: osimertinib 0.166 vs no-osimertinib 0.171, diff -0.005, p=0.1696. Interaction log-odds = +0.024, p=0.7584.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients on treatment_pembrolizumab, stk11_mutation=1 reduces the magnitude of treatment-associated improvement in objective_response (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: In STK11+: pembro 0.158 vs no-pembro 0.173, diff -0.015, p=0.0781. In STK11-: pembro 0.177 vs no-pembro 0.162, diff +0.014, p=9.16e-05. Interaction log-odds = -0.211, p=0.001785.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with keap1_mutation=1 derive less objective_response benefit from treatment_pembrolizumab than keap1_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: In KEAP1+: pembro 0.171 vs no-pembro 0.168, diff +0.003, p=0.7057. In KEAP1-: pembro 0.174 vs no-pembro 0.163, diff +0.011, p=0.002265. Interaction log-odds = -0.058, p=0.3625.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ albumin_g_dl); t-test mean by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin: responders 3.82 g/dL, non-responders 3.80 g/dL (t-test p=0.0001394). Logistic OR per +1 g/dL = 1.095 (coef=+0.091), p=0.0001398.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ldh_u_l (log-transformed) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean LDH: responders 224.3 U/L, non-responders 223.6 U/L. Logistic OR per unit log-LDH = 1.022 (coef=+0.022), p=0.5194.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr, log-transformed) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic OR per unit log-NLR = 0.980 (coef=-0.020), p=0.3573.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l (log1p-transformed) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

Markers of metastatic burden (liver_mets, bone_mets, has_brain_mets, stage_iv, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, pericardial_effusion, contralateral_lung_mets) are each associated with lower probability of objective_response.
Squamous histology is associated with lower probability of objective_response than adenocarcinoma.
Current and former smokers have higher probability of objective_response than never-smokers.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ current_smoker + former_smoker)  # never as reference
analyses.result_summary: Current vs never: OR=1.062 (coef=+0.061), p=0.1112. Former vs never: OR=1.034 (coef=+0.034), p=0.338.

proposed_hypotheses.text: sex_female=1 patients have a different probability of objective_response than sex_female=0 patients.
The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response differs by sex_female (interaction).
Probability of objective_response differs across race_ethnicity categories (relative to white).
Probability of objective_response differs across insurance_type categories (relative to private).
rural_residence=1 is associated with a different probability of objective_response than rural_residence=0.

analyses.result_summary: race_black vs white: OR=1.089, coef=+0.085, p=0.02088.
race_asian vs white: OR=1.086, coef=+0.083, p=0.0993.
race_hispanic vs white: OR=1.044, coef=+0.043, p=0.2075.
race_other vs white: OR=1.148, coef=+0.138, p=0.09351.
ins_medicare vs private: OR=0.975, coef=-0.025, p=0.3397.
ins_medicaid vs private: OR=0.926, coef=-0.077, p=0.03991.
ins_uninsured vs private: OR=0.986, coef=-0.014, p=0.8193.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Several comorbidities (autoimmune_disease, interstitial_lung_disease_history, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, diabetes_mellitus, hepatitis_b_history, hepatitis_c_history, hiv_positive, prior_immunotherapy) are individually associated with the probability of objective_response.
Higher symptom burden (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: fatigue_grade: OR per +1 grade = 0.978, coef=-0.022, p=0.04024.
pain_nrs: OR per +1 grade = 1.001, coef=+0.001, p=0.7755.
dyspnea_grade: OR per +1 grade = 0.993, coef=-0.007, p=0.4902.
cough_grade: OR per +1 grade = 1.012, coef=+0.012, p=0.2585.
appetite_loss_grade: OR per +1 grade = 0.990, coef=-0.010, p=0.352.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Markers of treatment-refractoriness or aggressive biology (tp53_mutation, prior_lines_of_therapy, prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_targeted_therapy, years_since_diagnosis) are each associated with the probability of objective_response.

Several oncogenic alterations (braf_v600e, alk_fusion, ros1_fusion, ret_fusion, met_exon14_skipping, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, her2_amplification, msi_high, cdkn2a_loss, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss) are each associated with the probability of objective_response.

None of the genotyped SNPs (snp_rs* columns) is individually associated with objective_response after accounting for chance (the count of nominally significant SNPs at p<0.05 is consistent with the ~5% false-positive rate).

analyses.result_summary: Tested 23 SNPs; 2 crossed p<0.05 (~1.2 expected by chance). Nominally significant: snp_rs7412 (OR=1.106, p=0.01822); snp_rs1050828 (OR=0.906, p=0.03116).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among routine labs (hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, glucose_mg_dl, platelets_k_ul, wbc_k_ul, anc_k_ul, alc_k_ul), several are independently associated with the probability of objective_response, with values reflecting better organ function/marrow reserve associated with higher response.

In a multivariable logistic regression for objective_response that includes the four treatments, their canonical biomarker interactions (treatment_pembrolizumab*pdl1_high, treatment_pembrolizumab*tmb_high, treatment_sotorasib*kras_g12c, treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation, treatment_osimertinib*egfr_mutation), resistance-marker interactions (treatment_pembrolizumab*stk11_mutation and treatment_pembrolizumab*keap1_mutation), and standard clinical covariates (ecog_ps, stage_iv, brain/liver/bone mets, albumin, log-LDH, log-NLR, log-CRP, weight loss, age, sex, histology, smoking, tp53), the targeted-therapy interactions retain large positive effects while several prognostic factors (ECOG, mets, LDH, NLR, weight loss, low albumin) retain independent negative effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within adenocarcinoma never-smokers, the treatment_osimertinib*egfr_mutation interaction remains positive and significant for objective_response, confirming the effect is not driven by histology/smoking confounding.
Among patients on treatment_pembrolizumab, squamous histology modifies the treatment effect on objective_response (treatment_pembrolizumab*squamous interaction is non-zero).

analyses.result_summary: Adenocarcinoma never-smokers (n=7075): osimertinib*EGFR interaction coef=+0.086, OR=1.090, p=0.5446.
